overview: This is a Rufous-capped Warbler (Basileuterus rufifrons), identified by its distinct rusty-red cap and yellow throat. These birds are native to Mexico and Central America, with small populations occasionally reaching southeastern Arizona. They are typically found in dense thickets and brushy habitats near woodlands. This is a Rufous-capped Warbler (Basileuterus rufifrons), identified by its distinct rusty-red cap and yellow throat.
These birds are native to Mexico and Central America, with small populations occasionally reaching southeastern Arizona.
They are typically found in dense thickets and brushy habitats near woodlands.

The Rufous-capped Warbler's most northern range reaches up into a few US states next to Mexico. This attractive bird can be found in the sparse underbrush.
Favors brushy and weedy areas adjacent to woodland and scrub. Usually in pairs or small groups which hop around low in brush with tails cocked.Read more
